AMAT.headline: Revenue flat at $7.0B both quarters; DRAM/HBM capex offsets logic/foundry softness — mix shift to memory continuing.

shifts.note: DRAM grew from 27% to 34% of Semi Systems; leading-edge DRAM inspection/deposition leading the shift
shifts.trend: Logic/foundry flat → memory taking share in WFE spend

shifts.note: Revenue flat YoY across both quarters — macro caution offset by HBM-driven memory capex
shifts.trend: $7.0B Q1 → $7.0B Q2 (roughly flat; YoY roughly flat)

shifts.note: No supply constraint language; demand-driven; customer qualification cycles ongoing
shifts.trend: No material change in supply commentary across quarters

shifts.note: Q3 FY26 guidance implies continued moderate growth
shifts.trend: Q2 in-line → Q3 guidance stable growth trajectory

AMAT.verdict: Flat revenue masks a bullish mix shift: DRAM (HBM-driven) is growing as a share of WFE, positioning AMAT well for the memory capex cycle. Logic/foundry softness is the offset. Thesis intact — WFE mix is rotating toward memory, which is AMAT's strongest segment.

AMD.headline: Data Center re-accelerating: DC revenue $16.6B FY25 → $5.8B Q1 FY26 (+57% YoY), outpacing overall company growth.

shifts.note: DC YoY growth re-accelerated from +32% full-year to +57% in Q1 FY26
Non-GAAP gross margin steady ~54-55%; operating leverage improving as DC scales
shifts.trend: Stable margin profile with increasing op leverage from DC mix

shifts.note: MI350 GPU + 5th-gen EPYC both ramping; annual Instinct cadence (MI450 next)
shifts.trend: Annual GPU cadence confirmed; 5th-gen EPYC server CPU gaining share

shifts.note: MI300X China exports ongoing; some caution on export control uncertainty
shifts.trend: No material China headwind disclosed yet in FY25; monitoring for FY26

AMD.verdict: AMD DC acceleration is the thesis — +57% YoY in Q1 FY26 is well above the FY25 pace. GPU and CPU are both growing, annual cadence is confirmed, and margins are holding. Key risk is export control uncertainty on MI-series to China.

AMKR.headline: Q1 FY26 net sales +27.5% YoY to $1.68B with all four end markets growing; capex stepped up 3x to $2.5-3.0B to fund advanced packaging capacity.

shifts.note: Broad-based recovery with all four end markets growing
Capex tripled from $0.9B to $2.5-3.0B guidance — largest in company history — for AI packaging
Arizona advanced packaging fab under construction for AI/advanced nodes
shifts.trend: US manufacturing footprint expansion underway

shifts.note: Margin compression expected as capex ramps before revenue; depreciation will rise
shifts.trend: Short-term margin headwind from $2.5-3.0B capex cycle

AMKR.verdict: Amkor is making a large bet on AI advanced packaging (chiplet, 2.5D/3D integration) with a 3x capex jump. The Q1 FY26 broad-based growth validates demand. Key risk is margin compression during the investment cycle and customer concentration in advanced packaging.

AMZN.headline: AWS continues +28% YoY growth; Q1 FY26 capex $43.2B (annualized ~$173B) — capex inflection is the story.

shifts.note: FY25 $128.3B capex → Q1 FY26 $43.2B alone (annualized ~$173B); AWS infrastructure and fulfillment both growing
shifts.trend: FY25 capex ~$100B (cash) → FY26 run-rate implies $160-180B+

shifts.note: AWS YoY growth stable at +28% across both periods
shifts.trend: AWS: Q4 FY25 $28.8B → Q1 FY26 $37.6B (steady +28%)

shifts.note: AWS operating margin expanded to 37.7% in Q1 FY26 vs ~36% in FY25
shifts.trend: AWS margins expanding despite surging capex — utilization rates high

shifts.note: AWS RPO $364B in Q1 FY26 from $244B at FY25 year-end — extraordinary demand signal
shifts.trend: AWS RPO: $244B (FY25) → $364B (Q1 FY26 = +49% in one quarter)

AMZN.verdict: AWS is the world's largest AI infrastructure spending engine. +28% YoY growth with 37.7% margins and $364B RPO signals durable demand. The capex surge ($43.2B in Q1 alone) is being absorbed at high margins — the hyperscaler capex cycle is accelerating, not decelerating.

ANET.headline: AI Ethernet solidifying — Q1 FY26 revenue +35.1% YoY to $2.71B; deferred revenue $6.20B signals strong future recognition.

shifts.note: Revenue growth re-accelerated: FY25 +28.6% → Q1 FY26 +35.1% YoY
AI Ethernet (800G/1.6T) becoming dominant product; AI Titans 48% of revenue in FY25
shifts.trend: AI-specific Ethernet infrastructure driving mix shift

shifts.note: Deferred revenue $6.20B signals strong forward recognition pipeline
shifts.trend: Product deferred revenue growing — inventory/fulfillment building for AI scale-out

shifts.note: Near-term revenue may not reflect full demand backlog due to product deferrals
shifts.trend: ANET cautions near-term lumpiness; long-term demand intact

ANET.verdict: Arista is the clearest structural beneficiary of AI-scale Ethernet networking. Revenue growth is re-accelerating, AI Titans are 48%+ of revenue, and deferred revenue provides earnings visibility. The main risk is concentration in 3-5 hyperscaler customers.

AVGO.headline: AI revenue re-accelerating: $12.2B FY25 → $5.1B Q1 FY26 (+77% YoY); 3 hyperscaler XPU customers shipping.

shifts.note: AI revenue growth re-accelerated to +77% YoY in Q1 FY26 from +150%+ FY25 base
shifts.trend: FY25 AI $12.2B → Q1 FY26 $5.1B (+77% YoY); on track for $20B+ run-rate

shifts.note: Custom AI ASICs (XPUs) for 3 hyperscalers ramping simultaneously in Q1 FY26
shifts.trend: 2 hyperscaler XPU customers FY25 → 3 shipping Q1 FY26 → more in pipeline

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 revenue guided $15.5-16.0B — continued AI revenue acceleration
shifts.trend: Q1 FY26 $19.3B → Q2 FY26 guided $15.5-16.0B (note: Q1 included VMware contribution)

shifts.note: China revenue contribution from VMware enterprise side stable; XPU export not flagged as risk
shifts.trend: No material China XPU export control issue disclosed yet

AVGO.verdict: Broadcom's custom XPU story is the most differentiated AI silicon play. Three hyperscaler customers now shipping, AI revenue run-rate approaching $20B, and more in the pipeline. Thesis: custom silicon replaces merchant GPU for hyperscaler inference at scale.

CAT.headline: Power & Energy +22% in Q1 FY26 to $7.0B; backlog jumped from $30B to $51.2B YoY — data center prime power is the growth engine.

shifts.note: Backlog $30B → $51.2B YoY; largest increase in Power & Energy on data center prime power orders
shifts.trend: Backlog growth dominated by Power & Energy (data center prime power)

shifts.note: P&E +22% in Q1 FY26 on data center prime power demand; guidance low double-digit for FY26
shifts.trend: FY25 steady → Q1 FY26 P&E +22% YoY

shifts.note: Tariff costs ~$700M in Q2 2026 expected — partially offset by pricing and surcharges
shifts.trend: New tariff cost headwind in FY26 not present in FY25

shifts.note: Strong margin performance despite tariff headwind — pricing power intact
shifts.trend: Solid margins across both periods; tariff offset in Q2 guidance

CAT.verdict: Caterpillar is a direct proxy for data center prime power buildout. Backlog surged 70% driven by P&E. Growth is accelerating. Key risk is tariff cost pass-through uncertainty, but pricing power has historically allowed CAT to offset headwinds.

CDNS.headline: Revenue growth accelerating from +14% FY25 to +19% Q1 FY26; China revenue $189M +36% — geography is a positive surprise.

shifts.note: Q1 FY26 +19% YoY — accelerating from FY25's +14%
Backlog grew to $8.0B from $7.8B — 53% expected in next 12 months
shifts.trend: Backlog growing with stable 12-month coverage ratio

shifts.note: China revenue $189M (+36%) Q1 FY26 — ahead of expectations
shifts.trend: China contributing to growth rather than being a headwind

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guided $1.50-1.55B — steady sequential growth

CDNS.verdict: Cadence is benefiting from AI chip design complexity driving EDA tool demand. Revenue growth re-accelerated, China is a tailwind not headwind, and backlog is healthy. The Ansys integration (at Synopsys) creates competitive positioning questions, but Cadence's AI-native tools maintain moat.

CEG.headline: Calpine acquisition closed Jan 7, 2026 — Constellation now world's largest private nuclear/gas power operator; AI data center nuclear PPA momentum accelerating.

shifts.note: $22B Calpine acquisition doubles natural gas capacity
shifts.trend: Pre-Calpine: largest US nuclear → Post-Calpine: largest US nuclear + largest private gas

shifts.note: Long-term nuclear PPAs with hyperscalers (Microsoft Crane TMI restart, Meta Oklo adjacent)
shifts.trend: Nuclear-to-datacenter PPA pipeline growing rapidly

shifts.note: Q1 FY26 first full quarter with Calpine — revenue/earnings step change
shifts.trend: Revenue step-change from Calpine consolidation; 80-year license renewals underway

shifts.note: FY26 adjusted EPS guidance raised; long-term upside from AI nuclear PPAs
shifts.trend: FY26 guidance above FY25 actuals on Calpine + nuclear premium pricing

CEG.verdict: Constellation is the clearest play on AI power demand meeting nuclear baseload. The Calpine acquisition provides gas peaking capacity while nuclear PPAs command premium pricing. The 80-year license renewal program is a long-duration moat. Risks: execution on Calpine integration, nuclear regulatory timeline.

CLS.headline: Q1 FY26 revenue +53% YoY to $4.05B with CCS +76% and HPS (hyperscale) +63% — AI server manufacturing demand surging.

shifts.note: Revenue growth accelerated from FY25 (+38% CCS) to Q1 FY26 (+76% CCS)
HPS (Hyperscale Products & Services) growing to 42% of total; AI server content per rack rising
shifts.trend: HPS now 42% of revenue and growing fastest

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guided $4.35-4.65B — continued sequential growth
FY26 capex ~$1B (~6% of revenue) for AI manufacturing capacity
shifts.trend: Capex expanding to support hyperscaler AI server demand

CLS.verdict: Celestica is a high-quality AI server manufacturing play with direct hyperscaler customer relationships. CCS +76% and HPS +63% in Q1 FY26 are exceptional. The mix shift to HPS creates higher content per unit. Key risk is customer concentration and margin pressure from AI server commodity content.

CMI.headline: Power Systems EBITDA margin 29.5% in Q1 FY26 (vs 22.7% FY25) on data center generator demand surge — margin expansion is the story.

shifts.note: Power Systems EBITDA margin expanded +680bps from FY25 to Q1 FY26
shifts.trend: FY25 Power Systems EBITDA margin 22.7% → Q1 FY26 29.5%

shifts.note: Power Systems +19% in Q1 FY26; data center generator orders driving growth
shifts.trend: FY25 Power Systems EBITDA +44% → Q1 FY26 sales +19%

shifts.note: Data center prime power demand remains strong; outlook maintained
shifts.trend: No change in demand narrative — data center build-out continues

shifts.note: Order book remains healthy; lead times extended on generator demand
shifts.trend: Strong backlog providing 2-3 quarter revenue visibility

CMI.verdict: Cummins is a pure-play data center power generator story. Margin expansion to 29.5% in Q1 FY26 demonstrates pricing power and operating leverage. The growth trajectory is strong and the demand outlook is unchanged. Risk: cyclical sensitivity if data center build slows.

COHR.headline: Datacenter & Communications segment growth re-accelerating: D&C +34% FQ2 → +41% FQ3; 1.6T transceiver orders scaling.

shifts.note: D&C segment re-accelerated from +34% to +41% YoY
1.6T transceivers scaling into production; >$200M+ order book building
shifts.trend: 400G → 800G → 1.6T product cadence driving content/unit increase

shifts.note: Gross margin expanding on D&C mix shift and operational leverage
shifts.trend: Margin improvement as higher-value AI optics take larger mix

shifts.note: No supply constraint language; demand-constrained environment with strong backlog
shifts.trend: Supply available; demand backlog is the gating factor

COHR.verdict: Coherent is a direct play on AI optics — 1.6T transceivers for hyperscaler AI infrastructure. D&C growth re-accelerated to +41% and the 1.6T transition is driving content expansion. The key question is whether CPO (co-packaged optics) eventually displaces pluggable, but that's 3-5 years out.

CRM.headline: Agentforce AI product launched Oct 2025 driving RPO expansion; FY26 revenue $41.5B (+10%) with FY27 guidance $43-44B.

shifts.note: Revenue growth stable at ~9-10% YoY; AI product launch has not yet changed growth rate
Agentforce AI agent platform officially launched Oct 2025; customer adoption being tracked
shifts.trend: New product: AI agents for sales, service, marketing workflows

shifts.note: RPO $72.4B (+14%), cRPO $35.1B (+16%) — forward visibility good
shifts.trend: RPO growing faster than revenue — future revenue visibility improving

shifts.note: Op margin 20% FY26 — maintained stable; AI investment being absorbed
shifts.trend: No margin compression from Agentforce investment; steady 20%

CRM.verdict: Salesforce is an AI call option: Agentforce is real and gaining traction, but growth rate of +10% hasn't yet re-accelerated. RPO/cRPO growth outpacing revenue is encouraging. FY27 guidance of $43-44B (+5-7%) is conservative — upside if Agentforce drives seat/consumption expansion.

CRWV.headline: Revenue doubling sequentially: $5.1B FY25 → Q1 FY26 $2.08B (+112% YoY); RPO surged from $60.7B → $98.8B.

shifts.note: YoY growth re-accelerated from 168% FY25 to 112% Q1 FY26 (on a larger base)
RPO from $60.7B (FY25) → $98.8B (Q1 FY26 = +63% in one quarter)
shifts.trend: RPO growing faster than revenue — enormous demand backlog

shifts.note: Microsoft 45% (down from 67%), Meta 20% — customer diversification starting
shifts.trend: Microsoft 67% FY25 → 45% Q1 FY26; Meta growing quickly

shifts.note: Substantial capex commitments outstanding — net losses continue
shifts.trend: Revenue growing faster than spend but losses persist; path to profitability key watch

CRWV.verdict: CoreWeave is the purest neocloud AI infrastructure play. Revenue doubling, RPO $98.8B, and customer diversification from Microsoft-only toward Microsoft+Meta are all positives. The main risk is customer concentration (45% MSFT), debt load, and whether GPU commodity oversupply eventually pressures neocloud pricing.

CSCO.headline: AI Infrastructure networking sustaining double-digit growth; Networking +21% Q2 and continued growth in Q3; AI-specific backlog growing.

shifts.note: Networking segment sustaining +21% growth into Q3; AI infrastructure driving demand
shifts.trend: Q2 Networking +21% → Q3 continued double-digit AI Infrastructure growth

shifts.note: AI Infrastructure (high-density switching for AI fabrics) becoming largest Networking sub-segment
shifts.trend: Traditional enterprise networking declining; AI infrastructure Cisco Nexus growing

shifts.note: Q3 FY26 gross margin guidance 66.0-67.0% — stable despite mix shift
shifts.trend: Margin stable as AI infrastructure has similar margins to enterprise networking

shifts.note: Q4 FY26 guidance implies continued moderate growth
shifts.trend: Q3 $14.9-15.1B guidance range → Q4 continuation expected

CSCO.verdict: Cisco's AI Infrastructure Ethernet is the established networking play for AI data centers. Growth is sustaining at +21% in Networking. The risk is Arista taking share at the cutting edge (800G/1.6T), but Cisco's installed base and enterprise relationships provide a durable position.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog $14.5B (+44% YoY); book-to-bill 1.2x in Q1 FY26 — data center power infrastructure orders accelerating.

shifts.note: Backlog +44% YoY to $14.5B; book-to-bill 1.2x means orders exceeding revenue
shifts.trend: FY25 total backlog $19.8B → Q1 FY26 Electrical Americas alone $14.5B

shifts.note: Data center power orders accelerating; Q1 FY26 organic growth strong
shifts.trend: FY25 Electrical Americas +16% → Q1 FY26 backlog +44% signals forward acceleration

shifts.note: Boyd Thermal acquisition closing Q2 2026 for data center cooling adjacency
shifts.trend: Expanding from power distribution into cooling — data center one-stop-shop

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guidance implies continued strong growth in Electrical Americas
shifts.trend: Strong demand outlook; guidance maintained

ETN.verdict: Eaton is a top-tier data center power infrastructure play with a surging backlog (book-to-bill 1.2x). The Boyd Thermal acquisition adds cooling, creating a more complete data center infrastructure offering. Risks: long backlog conversion cycles, tariff impact on manufacturing costs.

FN.headline: DCI revenue +90.4% in FQ3 on 1.6T transceiver ramp; total revenue $1.21B (+39.3%) — optical manufacturing capacity constrained.

shifts.note: Revenue growth accelerated from +35.9% FQ2 to +39.3% FQ3 YoY
DCI (Data Center Interconnect) +90.4% on 1.6T transceivers and laser chips
shifts.trend: DCI is the fastest-growing sub-segment at 90% — AI optics driving demand

shifts.note: Manufacturing capacity constrained; lead times extending for optical components
shifts.trend: Supply-constrained environment — capacity is the binding gating factor

shifts.note: Margins stable; mix shift to higher-value DCI products is positive long-term
shifts.trend: Favorable product mix shift maintaining margins

FN.verdict: Fabrinet is the manufacturing backbone of the AI optics boom. DCI +90% and telecom +42% confirm the demand inflection. Supply-constrained environment means Fabrinet's capacity is fully utilized. Risks: customer concentration in optical component OEMs (Lumentum, Coherent, II-VI).

GEV.headline: RPO surged from $150.2B → $163.3B in one quarter; gas turbine slots sold out through 2029 on data center power demand.

shifts.note: RPO grew +$13.1B in Q1 alone: $150.2B → $163.3B (+9% QoQ, +32% YoY)
shifts.trend: RPO compounding quarterly; gas turbine booked 110 units in 2025 (vs 68 in 2024)

shifts.note: Gas turbine manufacturing slots sold out through 2029; supply is the binding constraint
shifts.trend: Demand exceeds supply — sold out through 2029 on heavy-duty turbines

shifts.note: Gas Power EBITDA margins improving on mix and pricing power
shifts.trend: Margin expansion as premium-priced AI/DC turbine orders flow through backlog

shifts.note: RPO growth rate accelerating; Gas Power orders +62% FY25 vs FY24
shifts.trend: 110 turbines booked 2025 vs 68 in 2024 — 62% unit growth

GEV.verdict: GE Vernova is the single most supply-constrained energy equipment play on AI power demand. RPO $163.3B and sold-out slots through 2029 mean revenue is locked in and margin expansion is structural. The key risk is a data center build slowdown that causes RPO cancellations, but the booked/confirmed backlog mitigates near-term risk.

GNRC.headline: C&I (data center) demand accelerating: C&I +28% Q1 FY26; FY25 was down -2% — sharp recovery driven by large-megawatt gensets.

shifts.note: Total sales recovery from -2% FY25 to +12% Q1 FY26
shifts.trend: FY25 total -2% (resi solar headwind) → Q1 FY26 +12% (C&I data center + resi recovery)

shifts.note: Large-megawatt diesel gensets launched in 2025 specifically for data centers
shifts.trend: New product line targeting data center backup power — addressable market expanded

shifts.note: C&I +28% (data center) vs residential drag; new Energy Technology segment added
shifts.trend: Reorganized segments to highlight C&I data center vs residential distinction

shifts.note: OBBBA accelerated phaseout of solar tax credits will negatively impact resi/solar Energy Technology
shifts.trend: Regulatory headwind for Energy Technology (solar storage) offset by C&I strength

GNRC.verdict: Generac's C&I (data center backup power) recovery is the thesis. +28% growth with new large-megawatt gensets targeting hyperscalers. The residential drag and solar regulatory headwind are noise; C&I data center is the key business. Risk: generator market is more cyclical than turbines.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud +63% to $20.0B in Q1 FY26; capex $35.7B in one quarter (annualized ~$143B) — demand inflection accelerating.

shifts.note: Cloud YoY growth re-accelerated: FY25 +36% → Q1 FY26 +63%
Q1 FY26 capex $35.7B alone vs $91.4B full-year FY25 — run-rate significantly above FY25
shifts.trend: FY25 capex $91.4B → Q1 FY26 $35.7B (annualized ~$143B)

shifts.note: Cloud operating income $6.6B in Q1 FY26 vs $13.0B FY25 full-year — margins expanding rapidly
shifts.trend: Cloud op margin expanding: Q1 FY26 annualized >> FY25 full year

shifts.note: Total revenue $109.9B Q1 FY26 (+22%) — Search + Cloud both accelerating
shifts.trend: FY25 total growth ~10% → Q1 FY26 +22% acceleration

GOOGL.verdict: Google Cloud is the sleeper AI infrastructure winner. +63% growth with expanding margins and $35.7B quarterly capex signals that Google is all-in on AI infrastructure. The diversified revenue base (Search+YouTube+Cloud) provides stability while Cloud is the growth engine. AI-native search is a medium-term risk but also a tailwind.

HPE.headline: Juniper fully consolidated: Q1 FY26 Networking +151.5% YoY to $5.4B; AI server revenue growing; Juniper synergies $600M targeted by FY28.

shifts.note: Networking +151.5% on full-quarter Juniper contribution vs partial FY25 inclusion
shifts.trend: FY25 Networking +51.1% (partial Juniper) → Q1 FY26 +151.5% (full Juniper)

shifts.note: AI servers growing; Networking+Server combination positions HPE for full AI stack
shifts.trend: AI server demand growing alongside networking; converged AI infrastructure offering

shifts.note: $600M Juniper synergies by FY28 with $800M investment reaffirmed
shifts.trend: Integration on track; synergy pathway confirmed

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guided $8.0-8.4B — sequential growth from Q1 $9.3B (note: Q1 seasonally strong)
shifts.trend: Revenue trajectory normalizing as Juniper integration proceeds

HPE.verdict: HPE Juniper is now the most complete enterprise AI networking + server play. The +151.5% Networking growth is optics of full consolidation, but the underlying AI server and enterprise networking demand is real. Synergy execution through FY28 will determine whether the acquisition was accretive. Risks: integration execution, AI server commoditization.

HUBB.headline: Electrical Solutions +10.6% organic in Q1 FY26 on data center and light industrial demand; total backlog $2.3B.

shifts.note: Electrical Solutions organic +10.6% Q1 FY26 vs +7.1% FY25
shifts.trend: FY25 Electrical Solutions +7.1% → Q1 FY26 +10.6% organic

shifts.note: Data center and light industrial remain primary growth drivers
shifts.trend: Consistent demand narrative across both periods

shifts.note: Total backlog $2.3B — steady order book providing visibility
shifts.trend: Backlog stable; book-to-bill approximately 1x

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guidance implies continued moderate growth
shifts.trend: Steady growth trajectory; no material change to outlook

HUBB.verdict: Hubbell is a solid data center electrical infrastructure play with steady organic growth accelerating from 7% to 11%. The backlog provides near-term visibility. Not a high-beta story like ETN or CAT, but a quality compounder with data center tailwinds.

INTC.headline: DCAI +22% Q1 FY26 is a positive sign; Intel Foundry $2.4B operating loss persists — recovery narrative still fragile.

shifts.note: DCAI +22% Q1 FY26 shows AI accelerator traction after years of share loss
shifts.trend: FY25 DCAI recovery beginning → Q1 FY26 DCAI +22% YoY

shifts.note: Intel Foundry $2.4B operating loss Q1 FY26; restructuring underway under Lip-Bu Tan
shifts.trend: FY25 Foundry loss $10.3B → Q1 FY26 quarterly loss rate remains elevated

shifts.note: 18A process technology under intense scrutiny; only $307M external foundry revenue FY25
shifts.trend: 18A is make-or-break; external customer qualification ongoing

shifts.note: $4.5B in capex reductions announced; rightsizing for recovery path
shifts.trend: Capex cuts to stem cash burn during foundry transition

INTC.verdict: Intel is a turnaround story with high execution risk. DCAI +22% is encouraging but the foundry losses are structurally large. The thesis depends on 18A process success and external foundry customer wins. Until foundry losses stabilize, Intel is a high-risk, high-reward position. Not a core holding — more a turnaround option.

KLAC.headline: Record revenue $3.42B in Q3 FY26 (+11% YoY); Korea +80% on HBM DRAM inspection — memory WFE inflecting.

shifts.note: Record revenue quarter; Korea up 80% on HBM DRAM inspection tools
shifts.trend: Q2 FY26 $3.30B (+7%) → Q3 FY26 $3.42B (+11%) — acceleration

shifts.note: Korea now the fastest-growing geography at +80% YoY on DRAM/HBM
shifts.trend: Korea becoming dominant geography as HBM investment surges

shifts.note: Memory inspection gaining share; leading-edge DRAM chipcost/defect density requirements rising
shifts.trend: Memory mix increasing within Semiconductor Process Control

shifts.note: Services +18% growing faster than systems; services are higher-margin and recurring
shifts.trend: Services growing faster improves long-term margin quality

KLAC.verdict: KLA is the premium WFE inspection play, directly benefiting from HBM/DRAM complexity requiring more inspection steps. Record revenues and Korea +80% confirm the thesis. Services growth (+18%) improves revenue quality. Risk: escalating DRAM chip costs could slow investment, but HBM demand makes this unlikely near-term.

LEU.headline: Backlog grew to $3.9B (LEU $3.1B); $900M DOE task order for HALEU production; Fluor EPC contract signed — nuclear fuel cycle on track.

shifts.note: Total backlog $3.8B FY25 → $3.9B Q1 FY26 including $2.4B contingent HALEU
shifts.trend: Backlog growing as HALEU demand from advanced reactor developers increases

shifts.note: $900M DOE HALEU task order + Fluor EPC contract for Piketon enrichment signed
shifts.trend: Two major contract milestones de-risking the HALEU production path

shifts.note: LEU revenue $76.7M Q1 FY26 (+5%) — small revenue; growth is in backlog/options
shifts.trend: Revenue small and slowly growing; value is in the backlog and HALEU optionality

shifts.note: FY27 DOE budget does not include HALEU Operations Program funding — key risk
shifts.trend: Government funding uncertainty for HALEU Operations Program

LEU.verdict: Centrus is a long-duration nuclear fuel optionality play. The $900M DOE task order and Fluor EPC contract are major de-risking milestones for HALEU production. Revenue is small but backlog is growing. Key risk is DOE budget uncertainty for ongoing HALEU funding.

LITE.headline: Revenue +90.1% YoY in FQ3 to $808.4M; cloud transceivers +$137M; sole-source on 200G EML laser chips — AI optics demand surge.

shifts.note: FQ3 FY26 revenue +90.1% YoY — near-doubling driven by cloud and AI optics
Cloud transceivers +$137M; 200G EML laser chips sole-sourced — structural competitive advantage
shifts.trend: 200G EML sole-source position = pricing power and margin protection

shifts.note: Gross margin 44.2% (+1,540bps YoY) — dramatic expansion from mix shift to AI optics
shifts.trend: Margin re-rating as AI optics (higher ASP) replaces legacy telecom

shifts.note: Sole-source on 200G EML means supply is set by Lumentum's capacity
shifts.trend: Supply limited by manufacturing ramp; demand exceeds current capacity

LITE.verdict: Lumentum's FQ3 FY26 +90% revenue growth is the clearest demonstration of the AI optics surge. The 200G EML sole-source position creates a durable competitive moat. Gross margin expansion to 44.2% shows this is high-quality growth. The risk is losing the sole-source position as competitors qualify their EML chips.

LRCX.headline: Q3 FY26 revenue $5.84B (+9% QoQ); memory growing from 34% to 39% of mix on DRAM/HBM — memory capex cycle confirmed.

shifts.note: Memory rose from 34% to 39% of revenue as DRAM/HBM investments accelerate
shifts.trend: Q2 memory 34% → Q3 memory 39% of WFE revenue

shifts.note: Revenue growing steadily; WFE growth in CY2026 expected with AI driving higher DRAM intensity
Korea/DRAM customer spending dominant; Taiwan/TSMC foundry capex stable
shifts.trend: Memory capex (Korea) leading; foundry (Taiwan) steady

shifts.note: WFE demand driven by AI/HBM complexity; no supply constraints disclosed
shifts.trend: Demand-driven growth; no supply side issues

LRCX.verdict: Lam Research confirms the memory WFE capex cycle. Memory grew to 39% of mix as DRAM/HBM capex intensifies. The +25% H1 YoY growth is strong. AI-driven HBM complexity requires more deposition/etch steps, which is directly Lam's core business. Risk: China export restrictions on leading-edge process equipment.

META.headline: FY26 capex guidance raised from $115-135B to $125-145B in Q1; revenue +33% to $56.3B — advertising monetization funding the AI infrastructure build.

shifts.note: FY26 capex guidance raised twice: initial $115-135B → raised to $125-145B after Q1
Revenue +33% YoY in Q1 FY26 — advertising monetization showing AI-driven improvement
Llama AI models + Meta AI assistant integration across all family of apps
shifts.trend: AI features driving engagement → advertising pricing power

shifts.note: Capex surge will compress FCF margins; but revenue growth is offsetting
shifts.trend: Revenue growth outpacing capex relative to prior years

META.verdict: Meta is the clearest case of AI investment paying off via advertising monetization. Revenue +33% while raising capex guidance twice signals high confidence in AI ROI. The capex ($125-145B FY26) flows directly into NVDA/AMD GPUs and AI networking infrastructure. Strong hyperscaler demand signal.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions data center cooling +$130M YoY in Q3 FY26; spin-off of automotive segment to Gentherm by end FY26 — pure-play cooling thesis.

shifts.note: Climate Solutions data center acceleration: +$67M YoY Q2 → +$130M YoY Q3
shifts.trend: Data center sales growing sequentially within Climate Solutions

shifts.note: Reverse Morris Trust spin-off with Gentherm for automotive segment — becoming pure-play data center cooling
shifts.trend: Post-spin: Modine = Climate Solutions only (data center + HVAC)

shifts.note: Incremental $100M capex over next 12 months for US data center cooling capacity
shifts.trend: Capacity investment to meet accelerating data center cooling demand

shifts.note: Automotive drag removed post-spin; Climate Solutions has higher margins than legacy automotive
shifts.trend: Margin improvement expected as pure-play Climate Solutions

MOD.verdict: Modine is undergoing a strategic transformation to a pure-play data center cooling company. The Climate Solutions data center growth is accelerating and the automotive spin-off removes the drag. Post-spin, Modine's multiple should re-rate to reflect the cooling infrastructure growth story. Risk: integration complexity and cooling technology competition.

MRVL.headline: FY26 revenue $8.2B (+42% YoY); Data Center 74% of mix; custom AI ASICs (Trainium, TPU) are the growth engine.

shifts.note: Data Center +46% YoY for FY26; Q3 FY26 already showing +38% YoY
Data Center now 74% of total revenue — dramatically more concentrated on AI
shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 DC 73% → FY26 DC 74% — further concentration

shifts.note: Custom AI ASICs (Trainium for AWS, TPU for Google) ramping; third hyperscaler added
shifts.trend: 2 hyperscaler ASIC customers → 3 by FY26 — additional revenue stream

shifts.note: US export restrictions flagged as risk; 15% China revenue-sharing compliance requirement
shifts.trend: China risk increasing; may impact some customers

MRVL.verdict: Marvell's custom ASIC story (Trainium, TPU) is the thesis. Three hyperscaler ASIC customers shipping, Data Center at 74% of revenue, and +42% FY26 growth confirm the trajectory. The risk is export controls impacting China-related customers and the ongoing ramp risk for large ASIC programs.

MSFT.headline: Azure growth re-accelerated to +40% Q3 FY26 (from +35% Q2); commercial RPO $627B (+99% YoY) — AI bookings compounding.

shifts.note: Azure growth re-accelerated: Q2 +35%/+39% → Q3 +40%; AI services accelerating within Azure
shifts.trend: Q2 Azure +35% → Q3 Azure +40% — re-acceleration

shifts.note: Commercial RPO $625B (Q2) → $627B (Q3) — doubling YoY
shifts.trend: Commercial RPO +99-110% YoY — AI commitments locking in long-term revenue

shifts.note: Copilot AI business approaching $1B+ ARR; AI models available via Azure OpenAI
shifts.trend: AI business becoming material revenue contributor

shifts.note: Gross margins stable as AI services (higher-margin than traditional cloud) mix grows
shifts.trend: Margin stability despite continued capex investment

MSFT.verdict: Microsoft is the enterprise AI infrastructure and software stack. Azure re-accelerating to +40% with RPO doubling is extraordinarily bullish. The Copilot business approaching $1B ARR confirms enterprise AI monetization. Capex continues to build AI capacity that flows to NVDA/AMD. Core holding in the AI infrastructure thesis.

MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

MXL.headline: Revenue recovery continuing: FY25 +30% recovery from 2024 inventory trough; Q1 FY26 +43% YoY with Infrastructure optical leading.

shifts.note: Recovery from 2024 inventory correction continues to accelerate
Infrastructure (optical and wireless) driving growth, not legacy broadband
shifts.trend: Infrastructure +$36.3M Q1 FY26 on optical chip demand for AI networking

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guided $140-150M — continued modest sequential growth
Broadband recovering; Infrastructure accelerating; consumer electronics stable
shifts.trend: Multi-segment recovery with AI optics Infrastructure leading

MXL.verdict: MaxLinear is recovering from the 2024 inventory correction. The Q1 FY26 +43% YoY confirms the recovery is sustaining. Infrastructure optical chips for AI networking are the incremental growth driver. Risks: broadband spending cyclicality, customer concentration in a niche market.

NOW.headline: Revenue +22% in Q1 FY26 to $3.77B; RPO $27.7B (+25%); Now Assist AI agent deeply integrated — platform monetization accelerating.

shifts.note: Revenue growth consistent at +21-22% across FY25 and Q1 FY26
Now Assist AI agent deeply embedded; becoming standard upsell/renewal lever
shifts.trend: AI agents driving seat expansion and average contract value increases

shifts.note: RPO $27.7B (+25%) growing faster than revenue — forward visibility strong
shifts.trend: RPO growth outpacing revenue: +25% vs +22% = backlog building

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guided $3.77-3.82B — sequential growth guidance maintained
shifts.trend: Q1 $3.77B → Q2 guide $3.77-3.82B; FY26 subscription growing

NOW.verdict: ServiceNow is a high-quality AI software compounder. Now Assist is embedded in the renewal cycle, driving both retention and upsell. RPO growing faster than revenue provides forward visibility. +22% growth at $13B+ scale is exceptional for enterprise software. Core AI software holding.

NVDA.headline: Q1 FY27 revenue near/above $43B guidance; Blackwell Ultra (GB300) ramp; Rubin (B300 successor) disclosure; China optionality impaired.

shifts.note: FY26 DC +68% YoY; Q1 FY27 near/above $43B guidance implies continued >60% YoY
shifts.trend: FY26 DC $130B+ → Q1 FY27 $43B run-rate (full year $170B+)

shifts.note: Blackwell Ultra (GB300) ramping in Q1 FY27; Rubin architecture disclosed as successor
shifts.trend: Blackwell majority → Blackwell Ultra → Rubin (2027+) product cadence

shifts.note: H20 effectively zero; $4.5B Q1 FY26 inventory write-down; H200 25% import tariff
shifts.trend: China optionality fully impaired; modeled at zero

shifts.note: 4 customers each >10% revenue (22/15/13/11%) disclosed in Q3 FY26 10-Q
shifts.trend: Hyperscaler concentration is high; diversification limited

shifts.note: Data center, energy, and capital availability remain binding constraints on customers
shifts.trend: Supply language unchanged — AI infrastructure constrained, not demand

NVDA.verdict: NVDA remains the central node of AI infrastructure. Q1 FY27 near $43B (≈170B+ run-rate), Blackwell Ultra ramping, and Rubin in 2027 sustain the product cadence advantage. China impairment is fully digested. The key risk is hyperscaler in-house ASIC displacement (Trainium, TPU, XPU) — a 3-5 year risk, not 1-2 year.

NVT.headline: Q1 FY26 +53.5% revenue (organic +34.4%); Systems Protection +76% to $895M on data center UPS demand — momentum sustaining.

shifts.note: FY25 +30% organic → Q1 FY26 +34.4% organic — growth accelerating
Systems Protection (UPS, PDUs for data centers) +76% to $895M — dominant segment
shifts.trend: Systems Protection becoming 72% of total at Q1 rate

shifts.note: Backlog $2.3B with AI/data center cited as primary driver
shifts.trend: Backlog growing as lead times extend on high demand

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guidance implies continued strong growth; megatrends outlook maintained
shifts.trend: Data center megatrend language sustained across both reporting periods

NVT.verdict: nVent Electric is a high-quality data center UPS/power protection play with 34% organic growth. Systems Protection dominates and +76% in Q1 confirms the demand inflection is sustaining. Backlog provides visibility. Risk: concentration in large data center customers and potential pricing pressure as competition increases.

OKLO.headline: Order book ~18,100 MWe; Meta 1.2 GW prepayment agreement executed Jan 2026; HALEU deconversion JV with LEU formed — commercial milestones accelerating.

shifts.note: Order book ~18,100 MWe — extraordinary scale for a pre-revenue company
shifts.trend: Order book growing rapidly; Meta 1.2 GW is largest single commitment

shifts.note: Meta 1.2 GW prepayment agreement executed; HALEU JV with Centrus formed
shifts.trend: Pre-revenue milestones: customer commitment + fuel supply secured

shifts.note: Operating cash burn $80-100M FY26; cash runway monitored
shifts.trend: Pre-revenue cash burn; will need capital raises before commercial operation

shifts.note: NRC license review ongoing; first Aurora unit targeted for 2027
shifts.trend: License path advancing; first commercial unit 2027 target

OKLO.verdict: Oklo is a high-optionality, pre-revenue nuclear micro-reactor play. The Meta 1.2 GW agreement and LEU HALEU JV are genuine de-risking milestones. The 18,100 MWe order book is extraordinary for a company with no commercial plants. Key risks: NRC licensing timeline, HALEU fuel supply, and project execution. Position-size accordingly.

ORCL.headline: RPO surged from $523.3B to $552.6B; OCI +84% Q3 FY26 to $4.89B; capex 3x YoY — Oracle is the fastest-growing hyperscaler infrastructure.

shifts.note: RPO $523.3B (Q2) → $552.6B (Q3) — extraordinary AI GPU cluster demand
shifts.trend: RPO growing quarterly; $97.3B YoY increase (Q2) and continuing

shifts.note: OCI +68% Q2 → +84% Q3 — re-accelerating cloud infrastructure growth
shifts.trend: OCI: +68% → +84% YoY acceleration

shifts.note: 9-month capex $39.2B = 3x prior year; new ATM offering for additional capacity
shifts.trend: Capex tripling to build out GPU cluster capacity for OCI

shifts.note: Q4 FY26 guided ~$15.9B — continued strong growth
shifts.trend: Q3 $15.3B → Q4 guide $15.9B

ORCL.verdict: Oracle is the surprise AI infrastructure winner. OCI RPO $552.6B is staggering for a company historically seen as database software. OCI +84% growth and tripling capex confirm Oracle is building out AI GPU cluster infrastructure at hyperscaler scale. The GPU cluster offering (multi-1,000 GPU clusters) is the unique differentiator.

PLTR.headline: Commercial revenue +95% YoY Q1 FY26 outpacing government +54%; FY25 income from ops $1.41B (43% margin) — AI monetization acceleration.

shifts.note: Commercial +95% YoY Q1 FY26 — nearly doubling commercial business
shifts.trend: FY25 commercial +53% → Q1 FY26 commercial +95% — re-acceleration

shifts.note: Commercial now outpacing government growth; AI platform (AIP) driving enterprise adoption
shifts.trend: Government was 60%+ historically; commercial now leading growth

shifts.note: FY25 income from operations $1.41B at 43% margin — outstanding for enterprise software
shifts.trend: High margins sustaining as commercial scales without proportional cost increase

shifts.note: AIP (AI Platform) boot camps → enterprise pilots → production deployments → revenue
shifts.trend: AIP pipeline converting to production revenue at increasing velocity

PLTR.verdict: Palantir's AIP commercial acceleration is the thesis. +95% commercial growth in Q1 FY26 is exceptional. Government remains a durable base. 43% operating margin demonstrates exceptional operating leverage. The risk is that AIP deals are large and lumpy — growth rate can be volatile quarter-to-quarter.

PWR.headline: Backlog $48.47B Q1 FY26 (+10% sequential); RPO $26.24B (+10% QoQ); Electric segment data center and power grid driving record backlogs.

shifts.note: Backlog growing sequentially each quarter: $43.98B FY25 → $48.47B Q1 FY26
shifts.trend: Backlog growing $4.5B in one quarter; Electric segment dominant

shifts.note: Q1 FY26 revenues $7.87B (+26%) — accelerating above FY25 pace
shifts.trend: FY25 record revenues → Q1 FY26 +26% YoY acceleration

shifts.note: Electric segment growing on data center grid connection + transmission upgrades
shifts.trend: Electric segment becoming dominant; data center + grid modernization dual tailwind

shifts.note: Q2 FY26 guidance maintained; FY26 revenue growth guidance mid-to-high single digits
shifts.trend: Guidance conservative vs. backlog trajectory; beat potential exists

PWR.verdict: Quanta Services is the electrical infrastructure EPC play for data center grid connections and transmission. Record $48.47B backlog with +26% Q1 revenue growth is compelling. The book-to-bill >1x means future revenue visibility is excellent. Risk: labor availability and execution on large complex projects.

SNOW.headline: FY26 revenue $4.7B; RPO $9.8B (+46%); Cortex AI agents driving consumption growth — AI workloads beginning to monetize.

shifts.note: Product revenue +29% in Q3 FY26; FY26 full year $4.7B
shifts.trend: Q3 FY26 $1.16B product (+29%) → FY26 $4.7B total

shifts.note: Cortex AI agents launched; AI workloads running on Snowflake infrastructure
shifts.trend: AI feature set expanding; Cortex becoming revenue contributor

shifts.note: RPO $9.8B (+46% YoY) with 46% to recognize in next 12 months
shifts.trend: RPO growth outpacing revenue — consumption contract model

shifts.note: Net Revenue Retention expected to decline long-term as consumption model matures
shifts.trend: NRR declining from peak; AI consumption may re-accelerate it

SNOW.verdict: Snowflake is the cloud data platform play on AI workloads. RPO +46% and Cortex AI agents demonstrate the platform is still growing. The NRR decline is a concern but AI consumption workloads should re-accelerate it. Risk: competition from cloud native alternatives (BigQuery, Redshift) and Databricks.

SNPS.headline: Ansys deal closed July 2025 — Q1 FY26 revenue +66% YoY to $2.4B; combined FY26 guidance $8.0-8.5B; BIS China informed letter is key risk.

shifts.note: Ansys closed July 2025 — Synopsys now EDA + simulation in one platform
Backlog includes Ansys deferred revenue; integration ongoing
shifts.trend: Combined backlog improving; Ansys adds engineering simulation recurring revenue

shifts.note: BIS informed letter May 2025 restricting certain EDA tool exports to China
shifts.trend: China risk escalating — BIS restrictions could impact China revenue (20%+ historically)

shifts.note: FY26 guidance $8.0-8.5B for combined entity; Q2 guided $2.5-2.6B
shifts.trend: Integration progressing; guidance implies stable organic + Ansys combined

SNPS.verdict: Synopsys + Ansys is the dominant EDA + engineering simulation platform. The combined entity faces China export control risk (BIS informed letter) but the strategic position is unmatched. AI chip complexity is the secular growth driver for EDA. FY26 guidance of $8.0-8.5B is the new baseline. Key risk: China revenue impairment.

VRT.headline: Q1 FY26 revenue $2.65B (+30%); backlog ~$15B (+109% YoY); book-to-bill >1x — data center power and cooling demand sustaining.

shifts.note: Backlog $15.0B (FY25 YE) → ~$15B+ (Q1 FY26) — extraordinary level maintained
shifts.trend: Backlog doubling YoY (+109%); more than 5 quarters of forward revenue

shifts.note: FY25 +27.7% → Q1 FY26 +30% — slight acceleration in growth rate
Americas +53% to $1.81B with 27% margin — leading geographic performance
shifts.trend: Americas hyperscaler demand outgrowing EMEA and APAC

shifts.note: FY26 capex $425-525M reaffirmed — expanding manufacturing capacity for DC power/cooling
shifts.trend: Capex growing to meet surging demand; capacity constraint is the limiter

VRT.verdict: Vertiv is the premier data center power/cooling infrastructure play. $15B backlog (+109% YoY) with book-to-bill >1x means the growth is secured through mid-2027. The Americas hyperscaler channel (+53%) is the most profitable revenue stream. Risk: supply chain constraints, competition from Eaton/ABB in power.
